subject: [BUG] [powerpc] [next-20260216/17] nfsd: use-after-free in cache_check_rcu() triggered by sosreport on ppc64le

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the reported bug was previously addressed in v7.0, but there are multiple instances of the same use-after-free (UAF) class and a new patch series is being worked on to address these issues.

reviewer noted that they have an internal bug reporting the same stack trace on ARM64, and suggested using this platform to confirm the issues are fully addressed before merging new fixes
reviews.raw_body:
You didn’t miss it. We responded to that report in February with
a fix that you and Neil both reviewed and it is in v7.0 now.

That fix addressed one instance of this problem; there are actually
several more, all in the same UAF class. I have a patch series nearly
complete to address these issues.

The issue Misbah reports here is not the same as the February issue,
though it is very similar.

---

We have an internal bug reporting the same stack trace on ARM64. Now
that we have both a reproducer and a platform that is easily accessible
it will be straightforward to confirm these issues are fully addressed
before we merge the new fixes.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-05-01.patch_summary: This patch does not actually introduce any changes, but rather a response to a bug report. The developer suggests that the issue may be related to a merge commit on x86 drivers and recommends running tests with KASAN enabled to gather more information about the object being freed.
